title: bunium longipes subsp. minor
intro: Bunium longipes subsp. minor is a subspecies of the Bunium longipes plant, commonly known as lesser bunium or lesser meadow parsley. It is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Bunium longipes subsp. minor include its ability to act as a carminative, reducing flatulence and bloating, and its antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve muscle cramps and spasms. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
